rationale.R1.2: The text makes several logical leaps — most notably the inference that classifier disagreement signals novelty (supported only by a single percentage) and the attribution of ensemble gain to representational diversity. Checking whether premises support conclusions is plausibly load-bearing under a scientific-review reading.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims are present (63% disagreement-novelty rate, macro-F1 0.81, 4–7 F1-point gains, rare-class F1 of 0.55) but the artifact alone does not surface the underlying tables or ablations needed to verify them. Evidence–claim alignment is plausibly load-bearing under a scientific-review reading.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation ('take a look') is commonly used to solicit writing feedback; sentence-level clarity, transitions between paragraphs, and passive/active voice choices are all reviewable here. Writing polish is plausibly load-bearing under an editorial reading.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on scientific content — checking whether the evidence, numbers, and logical inferences hold up — or on writing quality — clarity, style, and prose polish?
